skill.name: docx-toolkit
skill.description: Create, edit, and format professional DOCX documents using the OpenXML SDK for .NET. Provides three pipelines: create new documents from scratch, edit content in existing documents, and apply template formatting with XSD validation. Supports complex layouts including tables, headers, footers, tables of contents, images, tracked changes, comments, and CJK typography.
skill.summary: Create and format professional DOCX Word documents with OpenXML SDK — tables, styles, TOC, tracked changes, and CJK typography support.
skill.author: Fokkyp

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 2894 potential issues across 75 files (38297 lines). After AI evaluation, the vast majority are false positives: XML namespace URLs in OOXML assets, markdown code-fence backticks in reference docs, C# System.* namespace references, Unicode escape sequences in string literals, and standard JPEG SOF marker constants (0xC0, 0xC2). The legitimate concerns are: setup scripts require sudo for .NET SDK installation, scripts download external resources (dotnet-install.sh from Microsoft), and the skill generates and executes .NET code as part of its document creation pipeline. No malicious intent or credential exfiltration patterns found. Safe to publish with appropriate user warnings about setup requirements.

medium_findings.title: Setup Script Requires Sudo Privileges
medium_findings.description: scripts/setup.sh uses sudo to install system packages including the .NET SDK. This is standard for environment setup scripts but users should review sudo commands before execution. The script installs dotnet-sdk via apt, dnf, pacman, or zypper and downloads dotnet-install.sh from Microsoft.

medium_findings.title: External Resource Download in Setup Scripts
medium_findings.description: Setup scripts download dotnet-install.sh from Microsoft's CDN and NuGet packages during build. These are legitimate, widely-trusted sources for the .NET ecosystem, but represent a supply chain dependency.

medium_findings.title: Code Execution Pipeline for Document Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill generates and executes C# code via dotnet run as part of its document creation workflow. This is its core legitimate functionality, but users should be aware that generated code runs with the same privileges as the invoking user. The skill reads sample code files and compiles them into executable document generation tasks.

low_findings.title: OOXML Namespace URLs in XML/XSD Assets
low_findings.description: XML style sheets and XSD schema files contain hardcoded http://schemas.openxmlformats.org URLs. These are required namespace declarations for OOXML standards compliance and are not network requests. All instances in assets/styles/*.xml and assets/xsd/*.xsd are standard OOXML namespace URIs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Reference markdown files contain triple-backtick code fences for syntax-highlighted C# and shell code examples. The static analyzer flagged these as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are standard markdown formatting, not executable commands.

low_findings.title: C# System Namespace References Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: C# code samples use standard System.* namespace imports (System.IO, System.Linq, etc.) which the static analyzer flagged as system reconnaissance. These are standard C# framework references, not reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sudo Usage in Setup Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/setup.sh contains multiple sudo invocations for package management. This is standard for .NET SDK installation across Linux distributions but requires user review.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Script Download
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/setup.sh downloads dotnet-install.sh from Microsoft's servers. This is the official .NET installation method but introduces a supply chain dependency.

content.user_title: Create and Format Professional Word Documents with OpenXML
content.value_statement: Manually formatting complex Word documents is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates professional DOCX creation, editing, and template application using the OpenXML SDK for .NET, supporting tables, headers, TOC, tracked changes, and CJK typography.
actual_capabilities: Create new DOCX documents with custom styles, tables, images, headers, and footers
Edit and modify content, formatting, and structure of existing DOCX files
Apply professional templates with XSD schema validation for compliance checking
Generate tables of contents, numbered lists, footnotes, and comments
Handle tracked changes (accept, reject, manage revisions) in documents
Support CJK typography with proper font fallback, line breaking, and character spacing
limitations: Requires .NET SDK 8.0 installation for document generation pipeline
Cannot visually preview or render documents without Microsoft Word or compatible viewer
XSD validation covers a subset of the OOXML specification focused on document structure
Template application depends on well-formed input documents with clean style inheritance

use_cases.title: Generate Reports from Templates
use_cases.description: Create professional business reports, academic papers, and proposals by filling content into pre-defined templates with consistent formatting.

use_cases.title: Programmatic Document Assembly
use_cases.description: Automate bulk document generation such as contracts, certificates, and letters by combining template styles with data-driven content.

use_cases.title: Document Format Standardization
use_cases.description: Apply corporate or institutional formatting standards to existing documents, validate compliance against XSD rules, and fix formatting inconsistencies.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new DOCX document for a quarterly business report. Include a title page, table of contents, executive summary, and three sections with headings. Use a professional corporate style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic document creation with structured content

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the attached contract.docx file. Update the client name to 'Acme Corporation', change all dates from 2023 to 2026, and replace section 4.2 with the new text: 'The delivery timeline is extended to 90 days from the effective date.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Content editing in an existing document

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Template Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the document draft.docx and apply the academic template style. Validate the result against the academic XSD rules. Ensure all heading levels, citations, and figure captions follow APA 7th edition formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Document with Tables and Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a research paper DOCX with an abstract, five sections, three data tables with merged cells, two embedded charts as images, footnotes, and a bibliography. Apply MLA 9th edition formatting with proper running headers and page numbers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced document with rich formatting elements

output_examples.input: Create a DOCX document with a title page, TOC, and three chapters about renewable energy
output_examples.output: Generated a 12-page DOCX document with professional formatting. The document includes: a centered title page with author and date, an auto-generated table of contents with tab leaders, and three chapters each with numbered headings, body text in 11pt Calibri, and 1.15 line spacing. A running header shows the document title on each page.

output_examples.input: Apply academic template to my research paper and validate formatting
output_examples.output: Processed the existing document through pipeline C. Applied academic styles: 12pt Times New Roman body text, double-spacing, 1-inch margins, APA-style running header, and hierarchical heading numbering. XSD validation passed with 0 errors. Found and fixed 3 style inconsistencies in the original document.

output_examples.input: Edit the sales proposal to update pricing table and client details
output_examples.output: Opened the existing proposal.docx, located the pricing table on page 4, updated all price values from the old rates to the new 2026 pricing schedule, changed client name from 'Beta Corp' to 'Gamma Industries', and updated the cover page date. The document structure and formatting remained intact.

best_practices: Run scripts/env_check.sh before each session to verify the .NET SDK environment is ready
Read the relevant Sample C# files before writing custom document generation code to use tested patterns
Start with pipeline C (template application) when you have an existing document with target formatting to replicate
anti_patterns: Do not write raw OpenXML markup manually — use the provided C# helper methods and sample code patterns instead
Do not skip environment validation with env_check.sh before document operations — missing SDK causes cryptic failures
Do not use CLI commands for complex structural changes — write direct C# scripts using the provided scaffold for tables, headers, and multi-section documents

faq.question: What software do I need installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need .NET SDK 8.0 or later. Run the setup script once with bash scripts/setup.sh to install all dependencies automatically.

faq.question: Can this skill create documents in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes CJK typography support with proper font fallback for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean text. Full-width punctuation, line breaking, and character spacing are handled correctly.

faq.question: How do I choose between the three pipelines?
faq.answer: Use pipeline A to create new documents from scratch. Use pipeline B to edit content in existing DOCX files. Use pipeline C to apply template formatting with XSD validation to ensure compliance.

faq.question: Does this skill require Microsoft Word to be installed?
faq.answer: No. Documents are created and edited using the OpenXML SDK for .NET, which works entirely without Microsoft Word. However, visual preview requires Word or a compatible viewer.

faq.question: Can I validate my document against a specific formatting standard?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes XSD schemas for aesthetic and business rule validation. You can run the validate command to check documents against these rules and identify formatting issues.

faq.question: What types of document elements are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports paragraphs, headings, character formatting, tables, images, headers and footers, tables of contents, numbered and bulleted lists, footnotes, comments, and tracked changes.
